name: Chinese Remainder Theorem
task: Create /tmp/eval_crt/crt.py with crt(remainders, moduli) returning the smallest non-negative x satisfying x ≡ r_i (mod m_i) for each (r_i, m_i) pair. Moduli are guaranteed pairwise coprime. Result is x mod prod(moduli). You may use pow(a, -1, m) (stdlib modular inverse).

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_crt/crt.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); C = mod.crt; assert C([2,3,2],[3,5,7]) == 23; assert C([1,0],[4,3]) == 9; assert C([0,0,0],[3,5,7]) == 0; assert C([2],[7]) == 2; print('OK')
